vulnerability.reference: Average fairness optimization disregards the tail risk of disproportionately high rejection for under-represented groups.
vulnerability.generated: Submitters from small communities often co-author with high-volume labs, making their papers indistinguishable from excessive submissions and defenseless against average-fairness trade-offs.

impact.reference: Authors from small communities become disillusioned, reducing future submissions and diversity of ideas.
impact.generated: Disproportionate desk-rejection denies them publication venues, reinforcing underrepresentation and stalling career progress in AI research.

dose_response.reference: Optimization-based desk-rejection mechanisms trading off conflicting definitions under model uncertainty enable average fairness optimization to disregard worst-case group fairness, which in the CVPR 2025 algorithm scenario exposes small research communities to disproportionate desk-rejection, leading to disillusionment and reduced diversity.
dose_response.generated: Optimization-based desk-rejection trade-offs under model uncertainty, through disproportionate desk-rejection of small-community submitters, trigger their papers' indistinguishability from excessive submissions and defenselessness against average-fairness trade-offs, leading to denied publication venues that reinforce underrepresentation and stall career progress.
